name: Software Development Team
description: A dev team that talks, argues, and decides together. Five specialists who think out loud and challenge each other to find the best path forward.

brief_templates.name: Technical Specification
brief_templates.prompt: Write a concise technical specification for THIS project. Analyze the actual codebase provided below — reference real files, real dependencies, real architecture patterns. Include: tech stack summary, architecture overview, key modules and their responsibilities, data flow, API surface, deployment model. Do NOT invent technologies or patterns that are not in the code.

brief_templates.name: Sprint Brief
brief_templates.prompt: Write a sprint planning brief for THIS project based on the actual codebase state. Identify: current features and their completion status, bugs or gaps visible in the code, next logical tasks to implement, testing gaps, documentation needs. Prioritize by impact. Reference real files and modules.

brief_templates.name: Architecture Review
brief_templates.prompt: Write an architecture review for THIS project based on the actual code structure. Cover: folder organization, module boundaries, dependency graph, patterns used (MVC, event-driven, etc.), scalability concerns, coupling issues, recommended refactors. Reference real directories and files.

brief_templates.name: Security Audit Brief
brief_templates.prompt: Write a security audit brief for THIS project based on the actual codebase. Analyze: authentication/authorization patterns, input validation, dependency vulnerabilities, secrets management, API security, file I/O safety, error handling that may leak info. Reference real code patterns found.
